item.title: Phasmophobia 1.0 Aims for Unprecedented Immersion and Fear
item.content:encoded:
Phasmophobia was always a bit of a dream game for me, as someone too meek to actually go out and search for ghosts, and much more content staying in the comfort of my own, non-haunted home. Needless to say, I put hundreds of hours into Phasmophobia when it first released in Early Access back in 2020. After what seems like a lifetime, it's finally nearing its highly anticipated 1.0 launch, promising to up the scares to a whole new level.

To that end, we attended a hands-off preview session at Summer Game Fest 2026, which first went over the details on a rework for Willow Street -- an iconic location from the game -- and finally what we can expect to see for the 1.0 release, slated for 2027.

Willow Street - Cat Trees and Horrifying Paintings

So, it's been a hot minute since I've played Phasmophobia. My usual group fell apart, and this is certainly a game that benefits from having a party of friends to play with. As I understand it, several locations from the base game were reworked, and Willow Street is next on the list.

The original Willow Street, along with other locations in Phasmophobia, used pre-made, purchased assets. That was when developer Kinetic Games was a one-man show, but now it has dozens of developers at its disposal. These reworks are hand-made with new assets while also retaining the "spirit" of the original location, no pun intended.

I always enjoyed the residential houses in Phasmophobia more than any other location, so to see Willow Street getting a rework is great. This rework has a notable Palm Springs vibe in the outdoors portion, and upon entering the house, we see that it's a very lived-in building with a story to tell.

Cat trees. They're everywhere. On the walls, around the room. Whoever lives here really loves cats. I never really thought much about the stories of locations when investigating them, but with so much more detail and character, it brings Willow Street to life. 

By and large, the floor plan is the same, though the rooms themselves are different. The cat-oriented room, for instance, but also the kitchen with its island counter. Going into the garage, it's a full-on gym with equipment like weights and a treadmill, while the former storage room is now a shower. 

Going into the basement, it has a distinct man cave vibe, with a big TV, a beautiful sectional, and even a full-service bar. There are some hiding places scattered around to avoid ghosts, but with Willow Street being a smaller house in general, tense counters are almost a certainty.

The most terrifying room of the house is no doubt the atelier, formerly the boys' bedroom. It is filled with sinister, dark paintings, showing that the artist is clearly a disturbed individual. And this seems to be the general vibe Kinetic Games is going for with their reworks and the future of Phasmophobia. They want locations to tell a story with bits and details, but they also want players to interpret it for themselves and unravel the mysteries behind the hauntings.

1.0 Aims for a AAA Audio Experience

Speaking of the future, 1.0 is on the horizon. This is where Phasmophobia's Summer Game Fest presentation really impressed me. We take a surprisingly deep, sophisticated look at some new gameplay features and enhancements that will definitely add to the horror element of the game. Namely, audio.

Sound was a large focus here, and CEO and Game Director Daniel Knight assures me that they are aiming for AAA-quality sound design. This is achieved by completely revamping and replacing all of the pre-existing audio in Phasmophoia. Kinetic Games aims for dynamic sound that reacts to the state of the game, spatial audio, and more.

To highlight a few impressive details, Knight mentions "in-depth randomization," which is to ensure that no two sounds are exactly the same, just like real life. Sound will also now react differently depending on the material properties of the room. The wooden nature of the rustic Bleasdale Farmhouse will be distinctly different from the cold interiors of the Prison. 

Vocal communication is also a key element to Phasmophobia, so when you're talking with a friend, it's going to sound as realistic as possible. The voice chat is now processed by acoustics, so the screams of your wimpiest buddy will now reverberate around a location. 

Redesigned, Creepier Ghosts

1.0 also promises to bring a redesign for Phasmophobia's iconic ghosts. While we've grown to love (or loathe) these phantoms, they are receiving redesigns to fit more cohesively with the game's narrative updates. The ghosts will be more distinct and are more or less their own characters, now.

Kinetic Games is aiming for something less zombie-like and more ghostly, and that's definitely reflected in some of the concept images that we saw. One example is the Patient 07, a ghost in some sort of asylum attire. She was experimented on, with long hair obscuring her face.

We saw a small snippet of scripted gameplay showcasing an encounter with Patient 07, along with the new audio improvements. It was a terrifying encounter as our character enters a house and creeps their way upstairs. A loud, repeating thud is heard, and in the darkness, a ghost is banging its head against the wall (I believe this is Patient 07).

We're discovered, and quickly attempt to escape. The ghost briefly vanishes and reappears, doing a very spooky crabwalk, to crawling on the ceiling, to ultimately catching us. Look, if all ghost encounters are going to be this terrifying, people are going to need an extra pair of underwear.

Other redesigns include a drowned woman in 1800s attire, a child in pajamas, and a charred man with burns all over his body. And while these all sound really cool and creepy, the original ghost designs were generic enough that they could pass off as different every time. "The old woman ghost" of yore is now a more distinct character, so it might feel weird to see these characters in multiple locations when hunt locations are canonically spread across the country. 

Still, Phasmophobia's new offerings are impressive. It was surprising to learn just how large the team had grown, and that work is definitely reflected in the improvements to both Willow Street (coming in July), as well as the ghost designs and of course, the incredible new audio coming in 2027. I'll be putting together a new squad, so I'm very excited to try it out.

Phasmophobia was previewed at Summer Game Fest Play Days.

Austin cut his teeth writing various  fan-fiction stories on the RuneScape forums when he was in elementary school. Later on, he developed a deep love… More about Austin



https://techraptor.net/gaming/previews/phasmophobia-10-aims-for-unprecedented-immersion-and-fear

item.title: Forget The Heists, I Can’t Wait To Spend Hours Dressing Up GTA 6′s Protagonists
item.content:encoded:
I’ve never played a GTA before. When GTA 5 came out, in 2013, I had just graduated college and was still only just emerging from a youth largely characterized by prudishness and strict morals that did not leave a lot of room for heist simulators. Fortunately for me and everyone who knows me, it’s been over a decade since then, and I’m a different person now: a person who, among many other facets, is committed to playing GTA 6. Let me shoot things! Let me do heists! Bring on the crime!

Bring on the—extremely hot outfits?!

Look, I mean it when I say that I’m going to play GTA 6 for the GTA 6 of it all, but with this latest slate of screenshots released by Rockstar for the upcoming game, I’m now convinced that I’m also going to spend hours just dressing up Jason and Lucia in sick fashion. I mean, just look at these guys:

That jacket and skirt combo? That shirt with those shades? Okay!

Oh my God!

Reader, I love her.

And there are so, so many more of these. They both look so good. This is a huge improvement over the protagonist trio of GTA 5 who, while dress-up-able, were neither as hot as Jason and Lucia, nor did they have as much fashion game as these two do.

I’m someone who will spend hours in character creators tweaking all sorts of little details if I’m given time to. I love changing the clothes of my Sims, my Pokémon trainers, heck, even my World of Warcraft character. I am unbelievably stoked for the huge amount of sick outfits on offer for Jason and Lucia, plus all the other customizable elements like hair, nails, and tattoos. It does, unfortunately, look like I’m going to have to shell out $100 in order to access them all, and I’m still a little unsure about that. But I’ve never felt this compelled to buy a Deluxe Edition of anything before, that’s for sure. It’s just so neat that Rockstar Games made such a cool dress-up game for us that also happens to have car chases and bank robberies attached.



https://kotaku.com/gta-6-dress-up-simulator-2000710085
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Vice Up Your GTA 6 With These Vice City–Inspired Pre-Order Bonuses
item.content:encoded:
You can’t have a new GTA game set in a fictionalized version of Florida without paying respect to the OG Vice City. With the firehose of Grand Theft Auto 6 information still flowing in advance of preorders finally going live tomorrow, here’s a look at the Vice City-inspired perks you’ll get when you preorder the game.

’55 Vapid Stanier

When you pre-order Grand Theft Auto VI, you’ll get an exclusive classic car, the Vapid Motor Company’s 1955 Stanier. The car looks like it was pulled straight off the streets of Old Hollywood, sporting whitewall tires and a vintage yellow and white paint job that almost makes the car look like a vintage taxi. The car comes with a weapon locker and a secret compartment for holding any ill-gotten gains—which will be all of them.

Vice City-Inspired Looks

The ’80s are back with exclusive looks for protagonists Jason and Lucia. Jason’s linen suit features the ice-creamy pastels that were iconic for the time and a haircut that would make Don Johnson only a little jealous. Lucia gets a gorgeous red sequin mini dress that was made for being in the club, as well as a luscious set of curls to complete the look.

A Tropical Weapon Skin

The final pre-order bonus pays homage to the King of Vice City, Tommy Vercetti. His iconic palm tree button-up has been turned into a weapon pattern you can apply to most guns.

A Free Month of GTA+

The last pre-order bonus is a free month of Rockstar’s subscription service, GTA+ because the first hit’s always free. With GTA+, subscribers get $500,000 of GTA Online cash every month, access to the Vinewood Club car showroom, free access to other Rockstar Games, and other perks that change with every month. Find out more details here.



https://kotaku.com/gta6-preorder-bonuses-vice-city-tommy-vercetti-2000709954

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1832: Hints and Answer for June 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Wordle challenges players to guess a fresh five-letter word each day in six tries or fewer. Whether you’re a beginner or a daily player, today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1832 for June 25, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, June 25, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1832 is a somewhat challenging puzzle. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 3.8 out of 6 guesses, which is almost a full guess lower than the 4.7-guess average for June 24.

General Wordle Hints For June 25

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It refers to people or things coming together as one.
  • Hint 2: The word is often associated with cooperation, harmony, and shared purpose.
  • Hint 3: It can describe a group working toward a common goal despite differences.
  • Hint 4: You’ll frequently hear it mentioned in speeches, communities, sports teams, and organizations.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1832 for June 25, 2026.

Starter Words For June 25, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • YOUTH
  • UNTIL
  • STING
  • INPUT
  • COUNT

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1832 On June 25, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: UNITY

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

UNITY is a noun that means “the state of being one” or “a whole or totality as combining all its parts into one.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1831 on June 24, 2026

Answer: QUEER

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1832

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads-up. The game on June 26 will be Wordle Game #1833.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

item.title: Sony seemingly enforcing "stricter guidelines" on PlayStation Store to remove the mounds of PS5 shovelware games accumulating there
item.content:encoded:
Should get a Trophy for it.

Sony appears to be implementing "stricter" rules for releasing games on the PlayStation Store in an effort to cut down on the amount of shovelware - low effort games - that are appearing there.

Afil Games is a publisher affected by this, and it announced on social media that its games are being removed from the PlayStation Store and that its future output will not arrive there either.

"We would like to share an important update regarding our releases for the PlayStation platform," Afil Games' statement on X began (via Push Square). "As many of you may already know, since the beginning of this year PlayStation has been implementing stricter guidelines for publishing games on its platform. As a result of these new guidelines and their incompatibility with our business model, PlayStation has decided not to continue its partnership with Afil Games for future releases on the platform."

Afil Games is the Brazilian publisher and developer of games such as Chico's Delivery, Chippy's Stash, and Cat Pipes - all legit bangers I'm sure. It has published dozens of casual games, many of which exist to pad-out players' Trophy counts. Many publishers offer Trophy hunters easy ways to earn the digital silverware in their games, which are often incredibly simplistic and easy to beat as a result.

But Sony has demonstrably gotten more serious about shoveling the shovelware away. This year alone, Sony targeted several offending publishers, removing thousands of their games across multiple waves of removals. This effort to remove "slop" games, as they're commonly known, seemed to begin last year.

"We would like to sincerely thank all PlayStation players who have supported us throughout this journey," Afil wrote. "Your enthusiasm and trust have played a significant role in our growth. We remain committed to bringing new experiences to our players through Xbox One, Xbox Series, Microsoft Store, and Nintendo Switch."

We've reached out to Sony interactive Entertainment for comment and will update this story if we hear back.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/sony-ps5-playstation-store-stricter-guidelines-shovelware-games

item.title: I Too Would Like To Spend All Day In The Woods Making Snacks
item.content:encoded:
Good Folk Cafe is a little slice of a simpler life

Published: June 24, 2026, 9:46 am

Since at least once a day I throw my hands in the air and declare “I am going to run away to the woods!” when I get too overwhelmed, I love living out that desire in a video game without having to give up my apartment. The demo for Good Folk Cafe lets me putter around the trees doing simple tasks, while also hinting at bigger mysteries. 

Good Folk Cafe is an in-development game by Irish studio Not Here, Not There and has a demo up on itch. You play as a person named Aoife who lives alone in a wooded glen, spending their days wandering the countryside collecting real-world plants. The foraging mechanic is a simple timing-based minigame, and once your inventory is full you take the plants home to identify them using your plant book–which, delightfully for me specifically, includes their names in Irish.

At night, you use the plants you’ve foraged to cook meals for various mythological creatures who come to visit you. (The “good folk” of the title is another word for fairies.) Ingredients have flavor profiles, and you have to try out combinations to see what might please each creature. It’s satisfying to chop up plants and arrange them in your bowl, then watch a lovely animation of the creature curling up to eat. A satisfied creature rewards you with materials like wood, nails, and bike parts you use to fix up Aoife’s home.

Fixing things up unlocks useful tools like expanded inventory for plants or a bike to travel further (though using the bike isn’t available in the demo), and also seems to invite a neighbor over to tell you stories. This bit of the game is particularly charming: the stories are meandering and funny in the unique style of seanchaí, with one even having people chatting in the background. 

The art is simple but lovely, in particular the quality of light as you wander the countryside. The description of the eventual full game suggests more of a narrative around Aoife, but I also just really enjoyed the contextless routines of her day, taking little walks and doing little tasks. There’s no release date yet, but I’m curious to see new areas and hear more stories in the full game.

Computer Was A Mistake

Using a Bluetooth controller on my PC has broken 007 First Light, which is apparently just how things work



https://aftermath.site/good-folk-cafe-demo-impressions/

item.title: Star Fox's Switch 2 Launch Day Update Has Arrived, Here Are The Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
That's why you never rush a systems check

We are just one day away from Star Fox's Switch 2 arrival, and Nintendo is ensuring that the landing goes as smoothly as possible with a launch day update.

The ver. 1.1.0 update is available right now, adding a handful of online features like Battle Mode, avatar settings and an easy difficulty option for the main campaign. The patch also implements a bunch of bug fixes, so everything should be up to standard on day one (that's tomorrow).

The full patch notes were shared on the Nintendo Support site, and we have gathered them together for you to check out below.

Star Fox Ver. 1.1.0 (24th Jul 2026)

General

The following features have been added:

  • Battle Mode
  • Avatar Settings
  • Easy Campaign Mode difficulty

Bug fixes

The following bugs have been fixed:

Campaign Mode / Challenge Mode

  • Fixed an issue where, on the level select screen, pressing the Achievements and Replay Mission buttons at the same time could, in rare cases, prevent the game from progressing.
  • Fixed an issue where, after passing a checkpoint and collecting three gold rings to gain an extra life, selecting Restart → Checkpoint from the pause menu and then selecting Quit from the pause menu could result in losing one life.
  • Fixed an issue on Meteo where repeatedly using boost and overtaking the boss would make the level impossible to complete.
  • Fixed an issue on Solar where repeatedly using boost right after defeating the boss and overtaking it would prevent the transition to the next scene and make the level impossible to complete.
  • Fixed an issue on Aquas where hitting a shell-protected Garoa using a torpedo would incorrectly add to the bonus hit count.
  • Fixed an issue on Meteo where hitting a Flip Bot with a charged shot would incorrectly add to the bonus hit count from the explosion.
  • Fixed an issue on Venom where, if your Arwing was destroyed and you restarted immediately after defeating the boss, the hit count from the boss defeat would be added.
  • Fixed an issue on Bolse where hitting an enemy ship with a force field using a charged shot would sometimes incorrectly add to the hit count.
  • Fixed incorrect challenge text on Solar: "Score 25 hits using bombs against enemies and rocks” has been corrected to "Destroy 25 enemies and rocks with bombs."
  • Fixed an issue in Sector X where the "Destroy 10 Proximity Mines with a single bomb" challenge would sometimes not be recognised as completed even when the conditions were met.
  • Additionally, fixed an issue where the challenge on Venom could be completed even after taking damage from hitting a stone statue.

A number of other problems have been fixed to improve the gameplay experience.

Will you be playing Star Fox on day one? Let us know in the comments.

[source nintendo.com]

Jim came to Nintendo Life in 2022 and, despite his insistence that The Minish Cap is the best Zelda game and his unwavering love for the Star Wars prequels (yes, really), he has continued to write news and features on the site ever since.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply

item.title: GTA 6 is “a single-player experience” with no mention of a new GTA Online
item.content:encoded:
When Grand Theft Auto VI launches on 19th November, it will not have a new GTA Online or multiplayer modes.

It’s an interesting distinctions that Take Two and Rockstar has made ahead of the start of pre-orders for GTA 6 is to describe it purely as “a single-player experience” in the press release and makes no mention of GTA Online, outside of describing perks for the GTA+ subscription service.

Not only this, but the PlayStation Store page specifically answers that “Grand Theft Auto VI is a single-player experience.” in its FAQ, and the game only has a single-player tag on the Xbox store.

So what’s going on here? Are we not getting a new GTA Online?

Well, this does track with the last couple of releases from Rockstar, with GTA V and Red Dead Redemption 2 both launching with just the single-player story for players to dive into, before GTA Online and Red Dead Online were added via an update weeks later.

Grand Theft Auto V launched on 17th September 2013, with Grand Theft Auto Online arriving a fortnight later on 1st October 2013. Meanwhile, Red Dead Redemption 2 launched on 26th October 2018 and Red Dead Online was first playable from 27th November. Strictly speaking, Red Dead Online’s full launch actually came on 14th May 2019.

The long-held expectation has been that Rockstar will follow a similar process for GTA 6, launching the single-player game first and with the multiplayer to follow. It could be a legal requirement for them to only market the game as a single-player game for that reason, and since GTA 6 has only ever been shown off using the single-player to date, they no doubt want to keep their powder dry until they’re ready to reveal the new GTA Online.

However, there is also the possibility that Rockstar is looking to shake up how GTA Online is presented to players. As the 2013 edition has been upgraded and remastered for later console generations, GTA Online has become a standalone purchase, and so too has Red Dead Online. But could Take Two be looking to branch it off from GTA 6 from the off, similar to how COD Warzone can exist semi-separately to the main series?

Source: Take Two, PlayStation Store, Xbox Store



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/24/gta-6-is-a-single-player-experience-with-no-mention-of-a-new-gta-online/

item.title: Some of our favorite Switch 2 accessories are cheaper during Prime Day
item.content:encoded:
Antonio G. Di Benedetto

is a reviewer covering laptops and the occasional gadget. He spent over 15 years in the photography industry before joining The Verge as a deals writer in 2021.

The Switch 2’s price is set to increase by $50 on September 1st, starting at $499.99, so if you’re purchasing one before that (or you already own one), now’s a good time to also save on accessories while you can. Amazon’s Prime Day has delivered many great game deals, but some of the best sales are also on controllers, chargers, and other related products.

Our own Cameron Faulkner has gone to great lengths to test many of these for the Switch 2. There are great options from well-known brands like Belkin, 8BitDo, and Samsung, but there are some lesser known options from the likes of EasySMX and Tomtoc that are also gems. Like the original Switch, the Switch 2’s hybrid handheld nature allows you to accessorize it in unique ways — some of which can offer very nice quality of life improvements for either on-the-go or at-home play.

Here are our top choices for Prime Day deals on a variety of Switch 2 accessories.

$40

The P9 is great if you need more storage for your Nintendo Switch 2. The microSD Express card offers sequential read speeds of up to 800MB/s, but only gadgets with a microSD Express card slot can take full advantage of the highest speeds. That includes certain cameras, tablets, and drones with a microSD USH-I slot.

$7

My go-to screen protector brand for years has been amFilm. Three glossy tempered glass sheets come in the box, and the tools make installation fairly simple. JSAUX’s model is just as good based on my testing, and just as affordable.

$6

In keeping up with some brands that offer faster installations, amFilm offers a two-pack of glossy protectors that includes a “OneTouch” tray. Remove the protector’s bottom film, attach the tray to your device, and then press gently on the protector until it’s fully applied.

$8

Let’s start with one of the most affordable options if you want to go the matte-textured route. AmFilm sells a two-pack of protectors at Amazon for $8.99. The set includes a plastic frame that serves as a guide to align the protector just right. JSAUX makes a similar product that’s the same price.

$10

AmFilm also sells a two-pack with a OneTouch frame for easier installation. Again, keep in mind that opting for matte over glossy will reduce glare, but at the cost of viewing angles as well as the full vivid quality of the Switch 2’s screen. That’ll be an acceptable tradeoff for some, but not all people.

$25

Tomtoc’s hardshell case for the Switch 2 features a soft touch and a slim fit; it also includes a flap that protects the console’s screen while providing space for up to 12 cartridges.

$24

A hard shell carrying case that stores the Switch 2, up to 12 game cartridges, and has a pocket perfectly sized for an AirTag.

$24

Jsaux’s protective case covers the front and rear of the Switch 2 as well as Joy-Cons. It has a cutout for the kickstand, and under its top lid are slots for storing 10 game cartridges.

$12

A rugged slip-on case for handheld use of the Switch 2 that also makes it more ergonomic.

$16

The 8BitDo’s latest adapter resembles a flash drive, but once connected to your Switch’s dock, it allows you to wirelessly pair it with a variety of PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo controllers.

$60

The Mechanism Gaming Pillow helps gamers stay comfortable for hours on end. Made from soft, high-performance Lycra, it holds tech up so their arms don’t have to, letting them play longer without arm strain. It also comes with a mount to accommodate one of many gaming handhelds, from the Steam Deck and Switch 2 to the ROG Ally X.

$23

The official Nintendo Switch 2 webcam is expensive, so we suggest getting this cheaper Logitech 1080p model instead.

$20

Anker’s 10,000mAh Zolo Power Bank is the epitome of an everyday carry. The clever USB-C charger can quickly supply up to 30 watts of power to phones and other devices, and it’s outfitted with a built-in 5.4-inch cable so you needn’t worry about grabbing your cord before heading out the door.

$28

Powerful power banks don’t have to be cumbersome. Iniu’s 10,000mAh P50 is about as thick as a deck of cards, but features 45W charging speeds, two USB-C ports, and one USB-A port. Its braided lanyard also doubles as a USB-C cable, providing everything you need to keep all of your electronics fully juiced on the go.

$42

The EasySMX S10 is our favorite Switch 2 controller for people who don’t want to spend twice as much on Nintendo’s option. It offers an excellent take on HD rumble, along with amiibo support and the ability to wake the Switch remotely. It features a swappable eight-way (circular) D-pad, and can also work with PC, Steam Deck, and mobile devices.

$48

A quality controller with adjustable tension TMR thumbsticks, a unique (and quiet) floating D-pad, and PlayStation-style stick layout.

$40

A compact and comfy gamepad with silent sticks and buttons. Its shape and curves feels much like Nintendo’s Switch 2 Pro Controller, but it uses drift-free TMR sticks.

$50

The Pro 3 features swappable ABXY face buttons and joystick caps, providing players with a highly customizable experience. It also features new L4 and R4 macro buttons between the triggers and its USB-C charging port, along with more durable and power efficient TMR joysticks. Read our review.

$9

A handy ethernet adapter for maintaining maximum download speeds and minimal online latency while using the Switch 2 in handheld mode.

Verge Shopping

Sign up to get the best deals on products we’ve found and tested sent to your inbox.

Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.

  • Antonio G. Di Benedetto


https://www.theverge.com/gadgets/951950/prime-day-nintendo-switch-2-accessories-cases-controllers-docks-chargers-deal-sale

item.title: Feast Your Eyes On Over 60 New Grand Theft Auto 6 Screenshots
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders begin at midnight, and while we might get a trailer tomorrow, Rockstar has already given fans more than enough to dissect with over 60 new screenshots of the game. The images primarily focus on the game’s Ultimate Edition perks, which include exclusive customization options for protagonists Jason and Lucia, as well as weapon variants, vehicles, and access to certain in-game stores. While the internet is in its feelings about having to pay an extra $20 to access all of these perks, the images do show just how much you can customize both characters with GTA6’s clothes, hair, and tattoo options.

Also in the image roundup is a look at the Vintage Vice City pack, which is a bunch of pre-order exclusive cosmetics that harken back to Grand Theft Auto: Vice City’s ‘80s setting. If you want to see them all, click through.

GTA6 will run you at least $80, with the Ultimate Edition bringing the price up to $100. Though the game will be released in stores, Rockstar has confirmed physical copies will only include a download code, so if you’re pre-ordering tomorrow and want a disc to put into your PS5 or Xbox, you’re out of luck.



https://kotaku.com/grand-theft-auto-6-gta6-screenshots-ultimate-edition-vice-2000709996

item.title: Grand Theft Auto 6′s PS5 Version Claims It’ll Offer ‘Near-Instant Load Times’
item.content:encoded:
If your mind casts back far enough, you’ll remember a time when the GTA series was always launched as a PlayStation exclusive. GTA III, Vice City and San Andreas all came to Sony’s console first, not porting to Xbox until anywhere from six months to two years later. It seems that some of that relationship still exists, as it’s now been revealed that the PS5 version of GTA VI will offer a whole bunch of unique features native to the PlayStation’s “immersive” hardware, and if New Zealand pre-order information is correct, it’ll also have a PS5 Pro Enhanced version. Oh, and the claim of “near-instant load times.”

PlayStation’s blog has posted a bunch of details about how the PS5 edition of Grand Theft Auto VI will use all the flashy widgets that too often go underused on the system. That means the DualSense controller’s haptics will be wiggling and jiggling throughout, along with the adaptive triggers (that still feel like wizardly magic to me) and built-in speaker getting in on the action. The rather excitable copy on the site reads,

PS5 puts you in the center of the game world with the DualSense wireless controller reacting to your actions and bringing the sights, sounds, and sensations of Jason and Lucia’s story into the palm of your hands. The controller’s haptic feedback offers responsive vibrations, while its adaptive triggers provide dynamic resistance. The controller’s integrated speaker also adds another dimension to key moments and interactions, with sound effects from the controller enhanced by haptic feedback.

It’s easy to dismiss these as gimmicky nonsense, but in truth I’m constantly surprised by how much difference those adaptive triggers make to games that use them well. I can take or leave the tinny speaker barking at me, but as someone who usually defaults to my Xbox Series S, I’m now pretty tempted to opt in for the PS5 version, and not least because Sony is claiming its “ultra-high speed SSD” will provide “near-instant load times.” So I guess I’ll be clearing all the space off the internal drive again, then.

Of course, we don’t know how this will compare to the Series X, which while not able to provide the clicky triggers and far more enhanced haptics, may do just as well for load times with its own SSD. But what it certainly won’t be able to do is match the extra oomph of the PS5 Pro, if an enhanced edition is really real. This rumor comes from the X account of renowned Rockstar reporter @videotech, who posted a screenshot from a New Zealand PS5 listing the game’s release (November 18 there, because NZ exists almost a day in the future), showing the “PS5 Pro Enhanced” tag.

BREAKING: Pre-orders in New Zealand confirms Grand Theft Auto VI is PS5 Pro Enhanced pic.twitter.com/A1R4fsQYP0

— ben (@videotech) June 24, 2026

There’s no official word on this yet, but if it’s true you can be sure Sony will be boasting about it as loudly as it can as the marketing ramps up over the next five months. If only the Pros didn’t cost an eye-watering $900.



https://kotaku.com/grand-theft-auto-6s-ps5-version-claims-itll-offer-near-instant-load-times-2000709958

item.title: You Can Pre-Order GTA 6 Right Now in New Zealand
item.content:encoded:
If you live in New Zealand, I know this is very specific, then you don’t need to wait any longer: You can pre-order GTA 6 right now.

There’s no sneaky New Zealand trick here, before you start asking questions. If you’re based outside of New Zealand, you’ve got a few more hours to wait until you can finally lock in your GTA 6 purchase.

Grand Theft Auto 6 Ready For Pre-Order Now

As you can see here from @GameRoll_, while it’s officially June 24 everywhere else, the time difference means New Zealand gets first dibs on pre-orders!

You can pre-order either the normal edition or the more expensive GTA 6 Ultimate Edition—which includes a lot of stuff, by the way.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Has Practically No Loading Screens on PS5

Once you’ve locked in your purchase, you’re all set.

You should be able to preload GTA 6 close to its November 19 release date, and the game we thought would never come out (and still could get delayed, you never know at this point) will be yours.

In the next 24 hours, every other country and region will be able to pre-order GTA 6.

Grand Theft Auto 6’s base edition will cost $80, and you’ll also have a dilemma over the game’s controversial physical edition issue.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 is a ‘Single-Player Experience’ With No Mention of GTA Online

If you’re not in the right region to pre-order yet, kill some time with GTA 6 Trailer 2.

Will you be pre-ordering GTA 6 as soon as you get the chance? Let us know your guesses through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

item.title: GTA 6 Will Be PS5 Pro Enhanced
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders have officially begun rolling out, with New Zealand players among the first to gain access thanks to the region’s midnight launch schedule. As fans pore over the newly updated store pages, one small detail has seemingly confirmed that GTA 6 will be PS5 Pro Enhanced.

The game has already been marketed as ‘Best on PS5,’ with Sony highlighting features such as the DualSense controller and 3D AudioTech as leading factors, with practically no loading screens stealing the show. However, the PS5 Pro version could scale things up to 11, further cementing PlayStation as the best place to play GTA 6.

What Will GTA 6 PS5 Pro Enhancements Look Like?

Images of the updated PlayStation Store page have begun circulating online, and alongside the option to finally pre-order the game, fans have spotted the PS5 Pro Enhanced tag attached to GTA 6.

Exactly what those enhancements entail remains unclear, but based on other PS5 Pro-supported titles, such as Resident Evil 9, players can likely expect improvements to both image quality and performance through PSSR. Many are already hoping the enhanced hardware will allow GTA 6 to achieve a stable 60fps while maintaining near-4K visual fidelity.

With prices rising for the PS5 Pro, if you don’t already have one, it’ll set you back a whopping $900. It’s up to you whether playing GTA 6 in the best possible way is worth that price, but until it comes to PC, that’ll likely be where it plays best.

  • Related: Everything Included In GTA 6 Ultimate Edition & Price

For now, though, Rockstar and Sony have yet to outline the specifics, meaning any discussion around frame rates and graphical upgrades remains speculative until an official announcement is made.

What isn’t speculation is the sheer amount of conversation surrounding the game. Alongside the PS5 Pro news, fans have been debating Rockstar’s decision to lock exclusive single-player content behind the Ultimate Edition, while physical media enthusiasts were disappointed to learn that physical copies of GTA 6 will contain a download code rather than a game disc.

Everyone expected GTA 6 to dominate headlines once pre-orders opened, and that’s exactly what’s happened. With launch day drawing closer and a price now confirmed, it feels like the industry has officially entered the GTA 6 era. Join in with the hype and give the second trailer another view below:

Do you have a PS5 Pro? Where will you be playing GTA 6? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 news, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.
